Note: Point Reyes Lighthouse brg NxE3/4E Mag 3 miles
6:17pm: Let go anchor in Departure bay.
Cape Mudge one point off starboard bow 3 miles.
Noon: Steaming from Cape Fox through Revillagigedo Channel to Hog Rocks at end of watch.
1:30pm: Stood out the western channel & through Eastern channel to new anchorage off Turning island at 2:40pm.
1020 underway with frequent stops for soundings for much of the day.
1730 underway. Stopped due to fog at 1755.
7:50pm: rounding southern end of Middleton Island.
2:10pm: Underway, schooner in tow. Stood up Prince William Sound.
7:15pm: Cast off schooner. Turned round and Stood down Prince William Sound.
4PM-8PM: Passed between Afognay and Marmot Islands.
Northern Kalgin Island brg SWxW3/8W Mag 10 miles
mid: Barren Island on port beam end of watch.
Cape Kalekta brg E Mag 10 miles
10:30pm: Sea Lion Rocks bearing SW1/2W distance 1/2 mile.
Position 1 mile  N Mag  of Sealion Rocks (2)
Egg Island (3) brg NExN Mag 2 miles
11PM: Cape Prince of Wales in sight ahead
1230 anchored off Cape Prince of Wales
Cape Lisburne brg 154 degs. Mag 10 miles

Ice_Log: 11:00PM: Lookout aloft reports ice on stbd bow at 11 o'clock
AM: Sighted ice pack on port bow abeam on horizon.
8AM to Noon: Land in sight on starboard beam & the main ice pack in the port bow, beam &  quarter.
4PM to 8PM: Steaming eastward through drift ice searching for derilict Schooner 'Jane Grey! 4:30pm: Hauled in Pat log on account of ice.
7PM: Light drift ice visible from crows nest bearing from East to North.
8PM to Mid: No ice visible from crows nest.
Mid to 4AM: ice visble from aloft to Northward and Westward.
3:10: Wreck of !Jane Grey! found with mast resting on ice floe.

Note: 0600 underway, then stopped and boarded ships.

Ice_Log: 8AM to Noon: At end of watch ice pack visible from 2 points off ? to ? of starboard bow distance about 5 miles.
4:15pm: Towed schooner into open water. Came to anchor at 4:30pm
Note: 1615 went ahead towing schooner to water. 1630 anchored.

Ice_Log: 8PM to Mid: Anchored twice during watch but got underway both times on account of field ice drifting on us.

Note: 1915 moving to leeward of Point Barrow.
2145 anchored. His bearings make no sense. Using WNW3/4W instead of ENE3/4E. He's in the lee of Pt. Barrow.

Ice_Log: 8AM to Noon: Ice blink to the Northwest.

Note: 1804 underway to west of Pt. Barrow

Ice_Log: 8PM to Mid: Ice blink to Westward.
Mid to 4AM: Ice blink to the Northward and Westward during watch, but no ice in sight from crows nest.
4AM to mid: No ice in sight.
mid to mid: No ice in sight.
4AM to 8AM: Strong ice blink to westward. Ice visible from masthead.
8AM to Noon: No ice visible.
Mid to 4AM: Ice in sight during last hour of watch on starbord beam.
5:30am: lost sight of pack ice.  Ice blink in sight from SW to N.
8AM to Noon: Ice blink in sight to Swd and Wwd.   Ice in sight to SE at 1130am.
Merid to 4PM: No ice in sight.

Note: 0740 anchored. Sent crew ashore to propect for coal.
1600 underway, west three miles to coal vein.
2015 bearings off Cpae Prince of Wales and King Island
Cape Edgecumbe brg N Mag 3 miles at 1630.
4pm to 6pm: Steaming through Whitestone Narrows and Olga Strait. 4:30pm: Anchored off Entrance Island at entrance to St John Baptist Bay
MERID TO 4PM: Steaming through Peril and Chatham Straits. Noticed black bouy No1 on reef to Swd and Nwd of Fairway Island.
Turnabout Island brg E Mag 2 miles
Cape Fanshaw brg NxW1/8W Mag 4 miles
8am - mid: Steaming in Frederick sound & Wrangell Narrows. Entered narrows at 10am.
0000: At anchor Mary Island anchorage (Revillagigedo Channel).
4pm: Steaming through Arthur Passage, at end of watch (6pm) entering Grenville channel, steaming to southward. Channel
8am-merid: Down Finlayson Channel 10:15 turned into Klewtoo Passage and anchored
